title: Accessibility
text: Accessibility Reviewed July 2026 WCAG 2.1 level AA target Everyone gets in A real page, not a PDF nobody can open. What we have built, what we have not yet fixed, and how to tell us when we get it wrong. At the arena Lenovo Center Sensory room A quiet space on the main concourse with adjustable lighting, seating and noise cancelling headphones available to borrow at guest services. Wheelchair and companion seating Available in every price tier, not only the upper bowl. Book by phone or through the accessible seating filter on the ticket page. Assistive listening Receivers at guest services, no deposit. The arena bowl is fitted with a hearing loop. Captioning All in-bowl announcements and video board content are captioned on the ribbon boards. Service animals Welcome throughout the building. Relief areas are outside gates 1 and 3. This website How we build Every page is keyboard operable, has a single main heading, visible focus outlines and text that meets contrast at level AA. Animation respects your system motion setting, and you can override it in settings. Found something broken? Tell us and we will fix it, then say so here. Report a barrier Known gaps Video clips in the play by play do not yet have captions. The seating map is navigable by keyboard through the tier buttons but individual seats are not yet selectable without a pointer. Standards We target WCAG 2.1 level AA.

title: Need to know, the 2026-27 schedule
text: Feature 18 July 2026 6 min read Need to know: the 2026–27 schedule Eighty-four games, one more road trip than last year, and a January stretch that will decide whether the banner has company. The schedule arrived on a Tuesday, which is how these things always arrive: quietly, in a spreadsheet, months before any of it matters. Two extra games change more than the arithmetic. A longer season means a deeper rotation, more nights where the fourth line decides the result, and a bench that has to be built for attrition rather than for one perfect night in June. The club has spent the summer saying exactly that. Opening night Florida on 29 September, at home, with the banner going up first. There is no gentler way to start a title defence than against the team you spent four years measuring yourself against, and no harder one either. Eighty-four games is not a longer season. It is a different sport. The January problem Six games in nine nights, all west of the Mississippi, ending with a back to back. Every season has a stretch like this and every season it looks harmless in July. It never is. Whatever the standings say on the first of February will have been decided in that fortnight. The rest is familiar: divisional games stacked late, a light February, and a final week that could mean everything or nothing depending on what happens in the four months before it. More from the desk

title: Storm Tracker
text: Offseason desk Updated daily, June to September 72 days to puck drop Storm Tracker A season is 84 games and a year is 365 days. This is the other 281: prospects, the affiliate, transactions and the history that made the building what it is. On this day 19 July Sample entries Nineteen July 2006 The parade Raleigh closed Fayetteville Street for the first Cup. Nobody in the organisation had seen a crowd like it. 2018 Brind'Amour hired A captain returns to the bench. Eight straight playoff seasons followed. 2026 Cup day, Raleigh Twenty years after the first, the second. The banner goes up on 29 September. Prospect pipeline Affiliate and juniors Sample data pending API Next in line Justin Poirier Right wing Signed 2026. Development camp standout. William Håkansson Center Entry level contract. Arrived in Raleigh in July. Aleksi Heimosalmi Defence One-year contract. Affiliate minutes expected. Zach Sawchenko Goaltender One-year contract. Depth behind Bussi.
